Top 3 sustainable yields: Realty Income, National Retail, and AGNC Investment, with yields of 4.8%, 5.1%, and 9.5%

pack.linkedin: I screened 50 stocks with yields over 7% and found only 3 with sustainable yields. Realty Income and National Retail passed the test, with debt-to-equity ratios of 0.3 and 0.2, respectively. AGNC Investment also made the cut, with a 5-yr dividend growth rate of 7%. In contrast, 61% of high-yield stocks have credit ratings below BBB, increasing default risk. What are your thoughts on investing in high-yield stocks, and how do you ensure their sustainability?
